name: Michigan First
description_short: Michigan First is a member-owned credit union with nearly 100 years of history, offering personal and business banking, loans, mortgages, and insurance to Michigan residents.
description_long:
Michigan First Credit Union has been serving the Michigan community since 1926, making it a well-established financial institution with a century of history. As a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union, the organization operates under the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) regulatory framework, distinguishing it from traditional for-profit banks. The institution emphasizes community roots and local service, particularly highlighting its Detroit and Grand Rapids presence.

Michigan First offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal and business checking and savings accounts, share certificates, personal loans, auto loans, mortgages (both purchase and refinance), emergency loans ("Minute Money" available in 60 seconds or less), credit card consolidation, business loans, and insurance products. The credit union also provides financial counseling through their "Financial Exploration" program, which offers one-on-one guidance with finance experts. Members can access services through online banking, a 365-day live local call center, and physical branch locations.

The institution distinguishes itself through its century-long community commitment, emphasis on local customer service (365 live local call center available any day, any time), and member-focused initiatives like a $100 refer-a-friend bonus program. They offer specialized programs including homebuying seminars, virtual credit education seminars, and a "First for Good" community service program. The credit union also provides Minute Money emergency loans for unexpected expenses and emphasizes personalized financial guidance rather than one-size-fits-all solutions.

As a credit union, Michigan First operates on a not-for-profit basis, theoretically returning profits to members through better rates and lower fees. However, the website provides limited specific information about rates, fees, or detailed product terms, making direct comparison with competitors difficult. The institution appears well-established and community-focused, but prospective members should request detailed disclosure documents before opening accounts to understand specific terms, APRs, and fee structures.
services: Personal loans and debt consolidation loans
Homebuying seminars and credit education seminars
pros: 365 live local call center available any day, any time for customer support
Emergency loans (Minute Money) available in 60 seconds or less for unexpected expenses
One-on-one Financial Exploration program with finance experts to guide financial goals
Nearly 100 years of history and community commitment since 1926
Member referral program offering $100 bonuses for both parties
Comprehensive product suite including personal loans, mortgages, business loans, and insurance
Free homebuying seminars and virtual credit education seminars for members
cons: Website lacks specific information about interest rates (APRs) and fee schedules
Limited transparency on loan terms, approval criteria, and eligibility requirements
No clear information about membership eligibility or field of membership restrictions
Minimal details about business loan products despite advertising them as available
No disclosure of deposit insurance limits or specific account features on main website
best_for: Michigan residents seeking personalized financial counseling and community-focused banking
Homebuyers in the Detroit or Grand Rapids areas looking for mortgage and education support
Small business owners needing business loans and comprehensive business banking services
Individuals seeking emergency short-term loans with rapid approval processes
diagnosis: Michigan First is best for Michigan residents who value community banking, local customer service, and personalized financial guidance from a established, not-for-profit institution. The main caveat is that the website provides limited specific information about rates, fees, and detailed product terms, requiring prospective members to contact the credit union directly or visit a branch to make informed comparisons with competitors.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Michigan First — NCUA-insured credit union in Lathrup Village, MI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
